title: astragalus atlanticus
intro: Astragalus atlanticus, also known as the Atlantic milkvetch, is a perennial legume plant native to the coastal regions of eastern North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its adaptogenic properties, which help the body to resist stress and disease. The medicinal properties of Astragalus atlanticus are attributed to its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The plant can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or capsule, and is often used to support respiratory health, boost energy, and enhance overall well-being.
